reviews.text: Wouldnt take my case but that's not the worse part. I never actually take to a lawyer. I talk to a secretary who had no clue about the law and told me she didn't have time to take down all my statement  very unprofessional!

For every need that we’ve needed to hire an attorney Underwood has very experienced and qualified associates. This firm was hired to do an “investigation” into Katy ISD, and in that report they slandered myself and another well respected attorney without interviewing either one of us, and while also naming me, a private citizen in a public report. If you fail to retract and correct the erroneous statement you issued, I will be filing a grievance with the State Bar of Texas against everyone involved.

Lorren is an amazing attorney. After my husband was rear-ended on the interstate, she worked with us for over a year to ensure he received proper medical care and that everything financially was handled correctly. She was dedicated, responsive, and truly cared about our case. We highly recommend her!

This attorney tried to use her court language to convince my 13 yr old minor to take a plea bargain. Also intentionally left the office door open and had her brother listen on our meeting about our case. Talk about breach of client attorney confidentiality. I’m so glad I was there. I called her out on it and she immediately told my son she didn’t want to be his attorney.

I watched her objecting and being over ruled many times in Family Court...I find it ludicrous and time wasting...she's also disallowing important info to be shared ..Judges are certainly aware of her.." I object" nonsense

Lorren was not my best friend in the beginning due to my circumstances and the reason she became my lawyer.  Now, she has spoken very highly of my fiance and myself in court.  She is definitely better than I first expected.
Amazing work!
Lorren is the hardest worker I know!
She did great job!!!

I was arrested in the east Texas panhandle and charged with a felony and a misdemeanor, Joe was able to get the felony dismissed and the misdemeanor charges down to a reasonable fine and no time. This guy is amazing!! Stayed in contact with me throughout the whole process and did one hell of a job literally keeping me out of jail and prison. THANK YOU VERY MUCH!!!!!!!!!

The only lawyer I've ever liked. He's truly invested in his client. He worked tirelessly for us. Never had an attorney call and research like he did. Total commitment for the cause. I've never seen a lawyer get mad for his client. When the D.A changed the pre-arranged deal he stood up mad and gave a Moses like speech to let his people go!!! Best outcome, he swayed the judge. Still celebrating 🎉
He really cares about his clients.
Great attorney really fights for his people! Would highly recommend if you're in the Amarillo area

Joe Batson's a straight shooter.

He's honest with you about the results and thorough with his process.

I went from facing 2-10 for a second degree felony, to paying a fine and receiving only a misdemeanor.

He's easy to talk to, they pick up the phone or get back to you every single time.

Thanks for helping me get back home Joe. Hire this guy if you need assistance in Amarillo!

I cannot say enough about Attorney Batson. My fiancé was facing extradition, and for weeks I couldn’t find anyone who would take his case seriously. Every door felt like it was closing on us—until we found Mr. Batson. From the very beginning, he listened, he explained, and most importantly, he acted.

While public defenders kept delaying and giving us no answers, Mr. Batson stepped in, worked directly with the prosecutor, and immediately started moving things forward. He was honest with us about the process, the costs, and the likely outcomes—and he delivered exactly what he promised.

Because of his dedication, my fiancé was released and came home in time to be with me and our children, especially with our baby on the way. He even went above and beyond after release, personally driving my fiancé to Walmart and making sure he had what he needed. That kind of compassion and commitment is rare.

Attorney Batson doesn’t just practice law—he fights for families. He made the impossible happen for us, and I’ll forever be grateful. If you want someone who will truly stand by you and get results, look no further.

I have a lot of respect for this man. If you ever find yourself in legal trouble in Amarillo—or anywhere in Texas—Joe will go above and beyond to help you. Talking about miracles here! It's not just about getting results though; he's honest about the costs and incredibly fair. Nice office, too!

Mr. Joe Batson. deserves an exceptional review and my sincerest gratitude for the outstanding job he did in representing me. his expertise, dedication, and unwavering commitment to my case resulted in a successful outcome, exonerating me from the false accusations I faced.

From the very beginning, Mr Batson, demonstrated a deep understanding of the intricacies of the legal system and provided me with sound advice and guidance. He meticulously analyzed every detail of my case and developed a strong defense strategy that ultimately proved my innocence.

Throughout the entire process, Joe Batson exhibited not only an impressive level of legal knowledge but also a genuine care for my well-being. He consistently kept me informed, promptly answered my questions, and alleviated my concerns, which greatly eased the stress and anxiety I experienced during this challenging time.

I cannot express enough how grateful I am for Mr Batson's relentless dedication and hard work. He went above and beyond to ensure that my rights were protected and vigorously fought for justice on my behalf. His commitment to my case was evident in his meticulous preparation, attention to detail, and tireless advocacy.

I wholeheartedly recommend Attorney Joe Batson to anyone in need of legal representation. His expertise, integrity, and compassionate approach make him an exceptional attorney. I am forever grateful for his exceptional service and the positive impact he have had on my life.

Thankyou Joe Batson, for your excellent work and for giving me a fresh start.

Mr. Batson is awesome. Words can't explain the impact he has had in my life and my case. Needless to say, he's a great person and a great attorney. If you're  looking for representation, Joe Batson is the one!
I called Joe Baston needing information about a the law concerning a traffic ticket.  He gave me great advice that helped me keep my license from being suspended.
Joe was awesome when I called him for advise on a legal issue I had didn't charge me anything and saved the day.

If you are looking for a great lawyer look no further ,you have arrived at your destination .Mr. Batson listened to the facts of my case and was able to take care of it with the best possible outcome for me .In my experience with Mr. Batson I found him to be very professional ,and someone that I could trust and that if I was was to find myself needing legal representation again it would definitely be my first choice to hire Joe Batson

Mr.Batson is an outstanding man.He is also the best lawyer I've known he isn't one of those lawyers that just push papers an collect a check. He will fight for the truth even if he has to do the investigation of the case himself. An that is exactly what he did for me an got my case fully dismissed. Some times things don't just happen over night but you can be confident that Mr. Batson is doing his job . Thank you sir an God Bless you an your family
Very knowledgeable and got a great outcome on my case!
Mr Batson! Thank you for your dedication & professionalism. I appreciate the legal work you did on my behalf! Always accessible, diligent , and dependable when it came to my legal needs. Thank you!
Too busy to even talk
His text are pleasant . Very good atterny

My family hired Mr. Batson to guide us during the biggest fight of our lives. He not only treated us with respect and honesty, but fought for us as though he were fighting for his own. He’s a tiger. We will FOREVER be grateful for him and his wife.

He’s ok when your case is going to trial but when after the final trial he doesn’t respond to anything, like in regards to the other half not paying their debts or the other half put kids in danger or when they violate my constitutional rights, He’s just trying to make another paycheck before he retires. Wouldn’t recommend.
Thank you Mike for all of your hard work. He is very professional and skilled to do everything to help!  I would recommend Mike to anybody!
Has always been there to help with every situation actually cares about you and family I suggest him to every one.

Trial. Would not call witnesses. Told the judge that I was trying to get him to lie. Would not get the audio//video admitted. Had to cut court early for an appointment in amarillo. Now they say I have to pay 1500.00 for this engineer. Straight railroad.  God will deal with you
Mike had been my attorney he had went above and beyond I would highly recommend him he is a good honest man and would use him again.
They helped me every step of the way! Could not have asked for better results Thankyou so much Mike and Tammy! If you need a good attorney and staff that actually care. Call these folks!

Did good the first year, after that I feel my $5000 retainer was a total waste. Especially the last time I went to visit him he told me he “couldn’t” get me back in court and his paralegal could not find my file because it was offsite when I asked for the remainder of my retainer back.  As a client I had to call multiple times to get a call back and feel like my case was on the back burner the whole time, as a client who paid a lot of money up front I feel I wasn’t given the representation in what should have been a “land slide” easy case for him.

Mike and his staff where very attentive and prompt in response in all my questions. Even though I was extremely stressed about my current situation they where very patient with me. He did an outstanding job handling my case.

Hands down one of the best and quickest working attorneys you can find . He does a swell job communicating with you around helping with his other clients , there's not enough words to be able to explain/ express what im trying to say

He took care of my problem and I didn't even get to shake his hand and tell him thank you yet.

Attorney for divorce, but was also supposed to be helping in step parent adoption. Allowed the father to terminate rights/support without final adoption hearing leaving me alone to support son.  I have been bounced back and forth by OAG court and his office for 10 years to find out he based it all on father's affadavit that there was a potential adoptive parent, my boyfriend at the time? Divorce was not supposed to be finalized wiithout adoption hearing, which maybe why the attorney cut contact and filed nunc pro 6 months later. No one will even try to fix this and other attorneys seem to think this was one of the cases that caused Texas to shift toward demanding 2 parent responsibility. It has caused enormous stress & suffering on our family, it ripped us apart for 3 years after. My son was cheated out of a father & due process.
Awesome attorney I was very blessed to have met him and for him to represent me
He is very helpful

I had a great experience with T.D. Hammons and his assistant. They were professional, straightforward, and genuinely took the time to explain everything clearly. So glad they are still in business!! They moved to another location but they still have great customer service and take care of your traffic ticket needs.. not to mention they have the BEST prices.. other law firms are charging an arm and a leg.

As unfortunate as it was to have to meet this gentleman, meaning I was on my way to court.  TD was THE BEST I could have ever hoped for.  If ever-o- ever I need legal representation again, this is the man I will be calling!!! And if I find out anyone else is needing a lawyer... This is the man you want!! Those one star reviews ... Nu-uh.. those guys have something else going on because for reals TD is hands down THE BEST LAWYER that is out there!! He's simply amazing!!! 👍
Got my case dismissed real good to work with answered my calls personally
Best lawyer in Amarillo.
Good
He's a great attorney and always helps out!
Terrific staff and zealous attorney for the defense. I have dealt with TD on many cases and value his opinion and insight into each case.

Call this office, and they told me unless I want to except the plea which amounts to guilty, even if I have all the evidence showing that I’m innocent, it’s not worth their time to take. Then the receptionist ended up hanging up on me telling me that she’s been in law for 31 years, but apparently I know more than her, when I was just trying to get advice based on things I’ve already learned.

This had to do with an officer that chose to maliciously write a citation in a form where no one else gets a citation for it. I know this by looking at all of the other citations for what I was written one for, and then i’m trying to get the police video from the cop car that shows everything that was going on. Which basically amounts to civil wrongdoing by civil rights standards, but since my ticket isnt worth their time financially, I received an extremely rude conversation from this office and then got hung up on.

All the billboards advertising the guys. Ignore them. They are only plea deal lawyers. If your actually innocent, don’t waste your time.

I have a desire to be a lawyer, to help people. Once I get my law degree, I will never be like people in this office.
Bill was amazing through every step of our adoption! If we adopt again, we will use him!
Mr. Hamker and Laurie N. made our adoption experience go so smooothly!  Thank you!

We have used him for adoption and court intervention. He and his staff are very professional and honest. Their knowledge of how the system works is fantastic. I absolutely recommend him and I will continue to use him as my attorney.
He has helped me through alot of cases,  from wrecks to adoption,  in my opinion his is the best hands down. I will definitely use him for our next adoption.

Best of the best!! He represented me in 2 adoptions. He was fast, knowledgable, compassionate & thoughtful. He made the entire experience easier than ordering a pizza. He sent me drafts of all paperwork to approve and ALWAYS took my calls. He & his staff even brought his for the kids at court. He changed all our lives for the better.
He did our adoption and was on top of everything even though Cps rushed everything
Mr. Hamker and his staff are so kind! They have been amazing in advising us in our legal issues and so affordable!

My cousin, who works with the Amarillo courts, told me I had to use Bill Hamker, that he was the very best! Thank goodness for the recommendation. Bill helped us so much through a very difficult custody battle when our nieces went into care with Child Protective Services. CPS was difficult to work with and we would have never gotten very far in the process without Bill's help. Honestly, I can't rave enough about how awesome Bill and his Office Manager, Laurie Nahrgang, are. We closed out the adoption in March after 17 months of battle. Bill didn't even charge us more than our retainer. His rates were very reasonable and worth every penny!!! A good attorney like Bill is hard to find and worth his weight in gold!

Mr. Hamker and Laurie were so helpful and amazing with our adoption! We highly recommend them!!
Randy and Lisa King

Mr. Hamker and Laurie (Secretary) always do an outstanding job. I adopted four children

Hamker and his staff are amazing people!! I recommend him for any adoption!!!!

He was also our attorney for my sons adoption over 20 years ago. He is honest, friendly and professional and reasonably priced! If you want the best attorney, use Bill Hamker! You won't regret it!
Mr Hamker and his assistant Laurie are great!! They are very helpful and Mr Hamker is a great attorney!!
Mr Hamker and his assistant Laurie are awesome!  Very knowledgeable and helpful!

Very rude and disrespectful. I was appalled by the way this lady spoke. Do yourself a favor and save your money and look for services else where, the amount of money we spent was not worth it.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for your review.  I am direct and upfront with all my clients.  I don’t lie or sugar coat anything; some people don’t like that until we have go into court and it works in their favor.  I refuse to tell people what they want to hear just to get a five star review.  Best of luck to you and your family in the future.

reviews.text: Diana is amazing! She saved me from going back to prison. Thank you Jesus and Diana for your hard work.

She was very rude and wouldn't let me present my case. I had already made two inquiries, one by phone and one in person, and when she looked at me, she said, "I already spoke with you on the phone," as if she didn't have time. The entire consultation was very uncomfortable; she was in a hurry to finish talking and kept saying, "We can discuss the same thing all day," in a sarcastic way.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Ruth:
Thank you for your feedback. You're absolutely right to say I'm sarcastic. I'm also very direct and honest about the cases I handle. Honestly, we had a very detailed conversation about your brother's case on the phone, and I answered every question you asked that day. I explained the complexity of the case and everything that could happen depending on the information in the reports. Later, you insisted on having another conference call about the same thing we had already discussed. You brought other family members along without explaining what we had already talked about on the phone. It's frustrating, and I do my best in every case. I know I didn't tell you everything you wanted to hear, but I prefer to be honest about the type of case and the difficulties each case may present, especially with the issues in this one. Each of you had the opportunity to ask more questions, and I answered each one. I'm not a rude person, but I also don't want to waste my time or yours. Thank you for this; I'll keep it in mind for the future.
Even so, I wish you luck with the case. Good day.

reviews.text: Diana came highly recommended but her employee was disrespectful. I would not recommend her services at all. The initial service I received was inadequate.
reviews.ownerResponse: I’m very sorry to hear that. Feel free to call me so I can get more information.

Diana Hathaway is your best choice for a defense attorney in the Amarillo area.  She was a  prosecuting attorney for the area so she understands what it takes to defend clients very well. she’s busy a lot with her many cases and may not seem like she cares, but she does care and it shows in her end results AND when it comes to representing you in court she will show up every time for you and get you the best possible outcome.  I can’t say enough good things because my personal experience was on point with her, but the fact is she is your best choice for defense attorney in this area.  Love you Diana! You’re a rockstar!

Very good seasoning

She is very unperfessional, very rude. I would highly recommend you never use her.She is the worst lawyer I have ever had to deal with. We were told to be at the court house in Canyon. She finally talked to us and we had to go look for her. She had walked by us and didn't even say anything. And it was 30 minutes.She had a serious attitude because I had questions. Then she walked us over to the clerk to pay the fees and they wouldn't let us without the paperwork and then we had to go look for her again and sit and wait until she was done with a court. She has no consideration of others.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Ms. Aly:
It is really easy to forget, sometimes, that we should treat others how we want to be treated. It is important to me to be as efficient as possible. When I walked in and headed to the attorney's office, it was to pick up your daughter's paperwork and that of another client. You did not need to "find me" as I would have been back with it once I had it. When I walked out of the office you were rude to me. I did not know who you were, as you did not come into my office with your daughter to review her case, which is why you were unfamiliar with the facts, had you done so, all of your questions would have been answered prior to your appearance at the courthouse. In the end your daughter's original case was dismissed, you got the letter you needed and were able to leave the courthouse as quickly as possible. My job is to do well for my clients, which I did in this case. It is not my job, however, to be treated as less than any other person because the court appointed me to help your daughter with her case. I try to treat others as I am treated. I do not take kindly to being yelled at or talked down to. I am glad that your daughter's case is resolved and I wish her the best of luck in her future. I sincerely hope that everything turns out well for her.

reviews.text: Very helpful. Was very detailed in our case. Great at communicating any updates.

Very Patient, honest, and dedicated to his job. I had an attorney before him that wasn't all that great and had quit in the middle of the divorce and child custody. Greg knew it was probably going to look bad on his case since I was losing in court but he still took me in and picked up back where I left off. He's helped me through everything in the process and is very communitive. If it wasn't for him I would've been in a deeper hole then I was before. He's helped me get more rights to my daughter and helped lower child support. I would recommend anyone to use him as an attorney because I can honestly say THIS is an attorney.
Highly recommend Greg and his team. He was incredible for us in a very difficult family law case.
This law firm has taken care of all our needs for several years and we love everyone that’s works there! Great lawyers and super helpful staff!! Would definitely recommend!!
Horrible. Not worth any money we paid him. Worthless
Very professional and helpful in understanding everything. Kind and understanding staff.  Honest legal advice.

reviews.text: Mr. Freeman and his staff have been available and courteous!  Mr. Freeman took on our case extremely late in the process.  He worked diligently and was very effective!  We highly recommend him if the need arises.  He and his office staff have been professional and friendly each time we’ve been in.  Fantastic attorney with an outstanding and supportive staff!
He was so helpful and made sure that we had everything we needed.
Very professional law firm and they take care of their clients.

Honest , hard working and dedicated to his clients. Mr. Freeman was always available and  explained the things I didn't quite understand. Worked hard on the clock and even after hours to make sure all things were done properly. Passionate about getting the results that were needed for my particular case. Always open and honest, never promising on anything but to do his part. Not only is Mr.Freeman one of the best attorney's in town he by far has the best staff in town.

He kept me in the loop every step of the way, as well as  taking the time to help me understand the process.  I would highly recommend Greg Freeman to anyone facing legal hurdles.

Side note: All of the staff were also courteous and very helpful when I had questions pertaining to my case if Mr. Freeman happened to be out of the office or in court.
David Russell is an skilled attorney and mediator, and he did an excellent job as a mediator in a difficult case.

Absolutely amazing attorney! Knowledgeable, responsive, and truly dedicated to his clients. He went above and beyond for me every step of the way. I couldn’t have asked for a better lawyer and highly recommend him to anyone needing legal help!

He absolutely didn’t care. Didn’t obtain any of my medical records. Not a one. Didn’t do any work for a winnable case. Poor communication when there was any. Of course the disability claim was denied. I asked for an appeal. He refused to mail or fax a single sheet of paper on my behalf. He then dropped me as a client a single day before the appeal time expired.

Save your money! This firm will only accept disability cases that have enough medical evidence, which is one of the most direct ways to qualify for ssdi. This firm does not care about clients, it only cares to make money off of other people's hardships. They also do a poor job of returning phone calls and practicing active listening skills.
Reapply for ssdi yourself and keep the 20% of your back pay!!
Horrible. Very unjust. Would not recommend

At first my experience with this place was very positive everyone was very sweet and helped me understand my situation, however after my case was over and I was denied every single one of them ghosted me. Not even a call to see how I was doing or advice going forward just a cold letter that said they'd no longer be helping me and I'd have to appeal on my own. Now that I am trying to reopen another case they are dragging their feet even though they dropped me like a dead fly.

Mr. Eldridge was my son's court appointed attorney recently and I am so thankful that he was. He went above and beyond for my son and was able to get him probation when several other lawyers told me there was no way that probation would be a option. If you are in need of a lawyer Tate Eldridge is the way to go.

I used Mr Eldridge almost 20 years ago - way before there was such a thing as reviews on the web.
I just saw his advertisement on tv and it occurred to me I should write a review on my experience with him back then.
He went above and beyond representing me in my disability case. I appreciated him so much and wanted to give him a 5 Star review all these years later. I would not  hesitate to recommend him.
Although this review is about 15+ years late, I wanted Mr Eldridge to know how much I truly appreciated him and his kindness in the handling my case.

Tate Represented me in my case and though he went to court for me he LITERALLY told the Judge "He is not disabled" when I actually AM. This was 3 years ago. I think my denial was LARGELY due to this.

Then when the Judge took 2 YEARS to make a determination, instead of appealing and fighting for me he dropped my case, so thanks to HIM I am no longer eligible for Disability, due to all my work credits expiring and the pandemic hitting closed all the offices so I cannot appeal either.

If I could sue him I would in a heartbeat, him and the Judge. reviews.text: You might think that filing bankruptcy is something you can do yourself - I'm glad I didn't make that mistake! This is a field that is rich in nuance, meaning that experience is king. Patrick has seen it all before and steered me adroitly through the minefield.

reviews.text: Absolutely exceptional! From start to finish, The Swindell Law Firm guided me through the process of my Chapter 7 bankruptcy with the utmost professionalism and care. reviews.text:
Bankruptcy is never easy or a good experience. However, our bankruptcy experience with the Swindell Law Firm was easy as anyone could expect. Mr. Swindell and his staff were friendly, professional, helpful, and understanding. They made the experience better than we could have expected. We looked at other law firms, some who were not in Amarillo, and most of them had a base price higher than Mr. Swindell. If we had it to do all over again, I promise you, we would not hesitate to call Mr. Swindell again. However, due to the education, we received through our bankruptcy, I totally believe we will never find ourselves in this situation again. Thank you to Mr. Swindell and all of his staff for all your help, kindness, and understanding. We will forever be grateful to you all for everything you’ve done for us.

CLM and SCM

We had a very knowledgeable and understanding staff that helped make the process of filing so less complicated.   They were here to answer all our questions and gave us reassurance in this process.   Thank you for all your help and advice.

Went into my bankruptcy case not having a clue of what I was really getting into. Patrick and his assistant made things easy with their explanations and going over all the necessary documentations with me that would be needed. By the time my case was filed and court dates set I had a full understanding of what was to be expected. Patrick was to the point and transparent from the start about the process which most attorneys don't do. Best part of having him work my case is the phone call the day before court to ease the pressure a bit by going over expected questions and outcomes. It helped out a lot as I was feeling stressed despite my case being an easy one. Overall a good experience and I recommend his practice.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you, Mina for your review. It's also good to know that the phone call eased your stress. You did great!

reviews.text: Patrick was wonderful! He takes care of you and listens to what you need and tells you everything and is honest. Very prompt will call backs. Chelsea was so kind and replies to my emails were quick and she never got tired of me even after all of my emails to her lol! 10/10 would recommend!!

Our experience with the Swindell Law Firm has been nothing short of exceptional. We previously tried filing bankruptcy with a large firm with a lot of ratings that were relatively high. However, the other firm was so busy that by the time they got back to your case a month down the line, they would forget everything that was said before and you would have to start over on every call. We backed out and luckily we found the Swindell Law Firm. Patrick and his legal assistant, Chelsea, have been amazing. They know your case, they are attentive and responsive. They made such a stressful situation in our lives feel simple. I am grateful we found them and chose them to handle our bankruptcy case.

Very professional. Kept me informed on every step of a very complicated process. I can't imagine trying to do this without his help. Office was personable and knowledgeable. Always available for questions.

Patrick Swindell and his team are by far the best! His knowledge and patience helped us through a very difficult and emotional time.

reviews.ownerResponse: We stand by our work. Cases sometimes can become more complicated than anticipated causing an increase in fees. After seven years, situations can change which may lead to the necessity of modifying prior court orders in cases involving children.

reviews.text: Through highs & lows this woman & her team & herself have been present with information, examples, encouragements, hard truths, options, & support. I highly recommend Cynthia Barela & her team. If I ever need someone in my corner again, I will 100% choose her.

Cindi Graham and her team are extremely professional and achieved more for me than I would have hoped for. She has been very frank and upfront with me and has been aware and respectful of my time and money. I am extremely grateful God led me to her. My experience was exceptional.

The amount of care and time that Cynthia and her staff take to ease concerns and work through legal issues is priceless. I have never had a team more willing to investigate and explain the processes in which we sometimes have to go through in the legal field. They get an A+ from me!
Found just what i needed in teacher supplies. The manager actually found it when I called and remembered the next day when I went to get it. I was impressed!

I'm thankful I entrusted my case to Mrs. Graham.  I appreciated both her candor and knowledge during this very difficult season in my life.  The price for her services are well worth the investment.  Your case will be handled correctly.  Piece of mind in family law matters is priceless.

Mrs. Graham Far surpassed any standards I had for an attorney. She represented me several times for different situations with my son. The first was over a custody battle which went very smooth. The second time though was not as easy with far more on the line as I was trying to protect my son from a dangerous situation. I placed my son’s well being in her hands, and she did not take the task lightly. She fought for my son like he was her own, and refused to accept any outcome that would put my son in danger. Her staff was compassionate and always there to answer any questions while reassuring me that my son would be ok. I owe My peace of mind and my sons safety to her. I highly recommend Mrs. Graham and am so very thankful for her and her staff.
Very Helpful and honest

I have known Cynthia for three years. She has helped me through the process of a custody battle in which I was awarded full and permanent custody. She has always been courteous, giving, and thorough the whole time. She is a fighter and I have learned that as long as I listen to the advice she gives then the outcome is amazing. I am truly grateful to know such a great person and lawyer, and I will always go to her for legal help as well as refer her to others!!!

I had a similar experience as previous review.  Staff was helpful and courteous.  Scheduling was quick and convenient.  I was referred to Mrs. Graham because she is a “bulldog”.  However, a $30,000 retainer too expensive of a bulldog for me.  I believe that the paperwork I provided was used as a screening to determine how much I would be charged which seemed suspicious, unfair, and unethical to me.  Once we spoke she was very intimidating and basically stated that I wouldn’t make away with much.  I was minimized and devalued in her evaluation.  Coincidently, she knew I’d make away with enough to pay the retainer, right!?  The situation seemed like I’d be pushed into settling or at least be underrepresented.  In terms of collaborative law, I was surprised to learn that she was not aware of and did not practice according to Section 6.008 defense of divorce suit and Section 6.505 request for counseling.  I didn’t want divorce but she did a good job of convincing me why I should.

I think a lot more marriages would make it if the system advocated for marriage instead of endorsing failure as a business...

went in to talk to her and she was rude and acted like she didn't want the case at all. They made me wait 3 weeks to get in and then moved my appointment 3 times. Then said it would be a 15k retainer fee and that i wouldn't get any of that back. Total waste of my time, and to top it off she talked to me like i was stupid. Very disappointed  in her.

BEWARE- DO NOT use Wade Byrd!! This guy is terrible. I gave him the large retainer, and he WILL NOT call you back. Wade is more interested in hunting/fishing than your case. I was in the middle of mediation with Wade paying $800/hr to everyone and he was literally planning a pheasant hunting trip with his friends. 1 day before the final court case he was out of town on a fishing trip with his friends and couldn't meet with me to plan the case!
Wade Byrd DOES NOT CARE about his clients!  You will be sorry if you use him!!

I am deeply disappointed with the legal services provided by Len Walker. Throughout our engagement, there was a consistent failure to communicate, with emails and calls ignored completely for six entire months before we chose to sever our partnership. This lack of responsiveness created unnecessary stress and left us uninformed about crucial case updates.

In addition, Len demonstrated a shocking lack of professionalism. He made no attempts to hide his bias towards his clients, would refuse to communicate with anyone involved in the situation, and left us hurt and confused about where the legal process stood. Attempts to address these concerns were met with dismissiveness and indifference.

Overall, Len displayed a concerning and disgusting disregard for client satisfaction and basic responsibilities associated with legal representation. I strongly caution against engaging his services, as my experience suggests a severe deficiency in professionalism and communication. Seek representation elsewhere to avoid unnecessary frustration and disappointment.

reviews.text: It is with great pleasure that I write this review regarding my experience with Attorney Brian Farabough, while navigating the muddy waters of inherited mineral rights. I had been contacted directly by a couple of companies that wanted to purchase the rights outright, but I knew nothing about the regulations and the process involved, so I contacted Brian’s office, before making any decisions. It wasn’t a lot of money, but I wanted to make sure it was handled properly. Brian went above and beyond to research the information regarding the mineral rights, volumes, values, etc.; and shared where he located the information. I was up front about wanting to pay hourly, having a limited budget and my willingness to do some of the work on my own, if it meant keeping within my means. Not only was he accommodating, but he was more than fair in billing his time. He initially spent nearly an hour and a half on the phone discussing my situation, available options, laws and regulations at NO CHARGE. Although he made recommendations about what he thought would be most beneficial for me over time, he also listened to my concerns and took the time to understand my circumstances and what I hoped to accomplish – and helped me finalize the necessary transaction.  He made himself available when I needed him to be and was very responsive – to both phone calls and emails.  He provided outstanding professional service and is a man of integrity. I would highly recommend this practice, and specifically Brian.

reviews.text:
I came to Abbot Law during an extremely difficult time and needed guidance through a complicated divorce process. From the very beginning, they were clear, thorough, and honest about every possible outcome and what each scenario could realistically look like. I truly appreciated how transparent they were, even when the information was hard to hear.

They were also very accommodating when it came to my financial situation and genuinely worked with me to help keep costs as manageable as possible. Having both male and female attorneys involved in my case brought valuable perspectives, which made me feel more confident and supported throughout the process.

Since I was living out of town for much of the case, they made things easier by offering frequent Zoom meetings, which helped tremendously. When the final court date approached, they were very upfront about the likely outcome and advised against proceeding. While it was heartbreaking news, it was realistic, and I felt that it came from a place of genuine care for my well-being rather than false promises.

Throughout my nearly three-year divorce, Abbot Law was attentive, knowledgeable, thorough, and empathetic. I truly felt that they cared about what happened to me and my case. I would highly recommend this firm to anyone needing honest and compassionate legal representation.

James Abbott and his team are by far the best legal team to have on your side. They went above and beyond and shown love, care and support to my family in the family matter case. Thank you Abbott law firm for the amazing ,friendly and welcoming service y'all have given us.

Our finger prints got sent to their email and Jerry and his staff let that email sit for 6 months! reviews.ownerResponse: Jamie, as I told you several times both in our initial consultation and during my representation,  adoptions are very expensive.  The costs of filing fees, services fee, home study fees, ad litem fees, just to name a few can often exceed $3,500 in an adoption, and that doesn't include attorney's fees paid to the attorney you hire in the process.   Unfortunately, this is a sealed file, otherwise I'd encourage anyone who might believe this review as accurate to review the timely filing of the motions and documents necessary for an adoption within the first three months of my representation. You are right though, I failed to represent you as I would any other client, and I treated you as a friend.  It costs to run a business and hire employees to aid in the best costumer service possible.  As you mentioned, you met your initial retainer of $2,000, but chose not to make a single monthly payment thereafter.  Any other attorney would have withdrawn, yet, I proceeded, and no it did not take four years. I'm sorry that you feel as though you were wronged, but I work just like you do, and I cannot afford to not only work for free, but to cover my client's fees and expenses, which is what you must have expected when you determined you would pay no more than $2,000.00 total.

reviews.text:
The individual answering phones for Brooks’ office today, who introduced herself as his secretary without a name, was extremely rude and dismissive. After providing my name, she immediately interrupted me, asked who I was calling for, stated the case was closed, and then refused to even review the email authorization that permitted her to speak with me. Instead, she hung up the phone.

When I politely asked her to be courteous after the first interruption, she continued in the same manner. This type of behavior is unprofessional, disrespectful, and a poor reflection on the attorney she represents. First impressions matter, and it is disappointing that someone with this attitude is the initial point of contact for clients or colleagues.

I would expect better professionalism and courtesy from a firm that wishes to be taken seriously. Do better.

Formal Response Regarding Client Communication
To: Barfield Law Firm
From: Cleavie Figueroa
Date: October 1, 2025
Subject: Formal Notice of Dissatisfaction and Request for Correction Regarding Communication Protocol

This communication serves as a formal reply to your google response 10/01/2025 around 10:55 AM, concerning the original one star google review.

I acknowledge your statement regarding our purported "frustration" and your general assertion about appropriate conduct toward your staff. However, your response fundamentally misses the core issue of my inquiry and contains demonstrably inaccurate and unprofessional characterizations of my conduct.

Specifically:
1.	Inaccurate Assumption and False Accusation: Your communication included an assumption regarding my "frustration" and a subsequent, unsubstantiated accusation of verbal abuse. I categorically deny any instance of verbal assault or abuse directed toward any member of your staff. This allegation is unwarranted, unsupported by evidence, and constitutes an unprofessional and defamatory reaction to a request for transparent and timely legal communication.

2.	Failure to Address Substance: The central purpose of my prior communication was to address deficiencies in client service, case transparency, and prompt communication. Your response focused solely on an alleged conduct issue while entirely failing to address the substantive deficiencies in representation and communication protocols that initiated this exchange.

Barfield Law Firm’s responsibility is to provide professional, effective, and ethically sound legal counsel. The pattern of communication, culminating in this most recent and inappropriate response, is unacceptable and reflects negatively on your adherence to professional standards.

For context, public records indicate that Barfield Law maintains a Google rating of 3.3 stars, which suggests that the concerns I have raised regarding client satisfaction and professional conduct are not isolated incidents but rather reflective of a broader pattern of service deficiency.

I formally request the following:
1.	A retraction of the false accusation of verbal abuse.
2.	An immediate shift in focus to the substantive matter of the individual’s case and a clear, professional communication plan moving forward.
3.	Designation of a specific, professional point of contact capable of maintaining communication that is accurate, timely, and respectful.

All future communications will be formally documented, and this firm's conduct, including the unsubstantiated accusations contained in your last message, will be evaluated in the context of professional responsibility.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Cleavie,

While we understand that legal matters can be stressful and emotional, it's important to maintain professional and respectful communication at all times.
We're entitled to the same courtesy and professionalism that we extend to every caller.

Our client has indeed provided authorization for us to communicate certain updates with you.
BUT, we also reserve the right to discontinue that communication with you at any time if we're subjected to verbal abuse or disrespect.
We will absolutely not tolerate any behavior that's abusive or threatening, and we expect all interactions to be conducted with mutual respect.

As a reputable law firm with years of proven experience we're bound by strict attorney–client confidentiality rules under both federal law and the Texas Disciplinary Rules of Professional Conduct. This means that, even with authorization, we communicate only within the limits of the consent provided and cannot discuss case details beyond what is permitted.

We ask that all future communication with our firm remain courteous and professional so that we can continue to serve our client effectively and respectfully.

We strongly believe in the Golden Rule.

Barfield Law Firm

I began to use Bill Cornett as my attorney in 2019. I was in need of a will, a power of attorney and a medical power of attorney and Bill had been recommended to me by a family member. I met with Bill on several occasion to finalize what I wanted in my will and to name a power of attorney and a medical power of attorney. Bill was available to meet me and kept his appointments on time. The price  Bill charged me for his services were very reasonable. I have recommended to all my friends who don't have a will to contact Bill Cornett's office and make an appointment to see him. My mother died in 2020 and Bill had handled her will. My brother and I met with Bill and asked him to handle mom's estate. Bill set up time a time to conduct a Zoom meeting in his office with the County Judge to probate the will. Bill has since handled the transfer of my mom's house, car and property for the family. I felt the price that Bill charged to handle this was also reasonable. Recently Bill has been helping me with a finance company of California to recover assets left to my deceased older brother. Bill handled this by a phone call and an e-mail. I was having no luck handling it on my own. I met Bill years ago when I was actively employed in the Criminal Justice system but only a professional level. I have thoroughly enjoyed my meetings with Bill to use his services. Bill has always treated me with respect and has always been courteous. Bill was up front on the price he charges for his professional services which I appreciated. Thankfully I have only had to use Bill for Civil purpose and not criminal, but I would highly recommend him to anyone looking for a good attorney who is willing to help you with your problem. Bill has my trust and thanks. I will continue to use him in the future for any legal problem.

reviews.ownerResponse: Mr. Blount, You said I did not remember you,  but in fact I remember you well.   I represented you not “a couple of years ago”, but in fact in 2009 to 2010 in a divorce case.   You wanted custody of your two young children and I explained in the beginning that the case would be both expensive to try and would be a battle we might not win.  We hired an investigator,  developed some evidence favorable to your case and tried the case before a jury.   We had many chances to settle the case before you spent much on attorney fees, both before mediation and at mediation, however you insisted on a jury trial.   The jury did not see the case your way.   You are right it could have been much less expensive if you had followed my advice and not insisted on a jury trial.   You wanted your day in court contrary to my advice and despite a great deal of preparation on your case we did not prevail.  Your complaint about the money you spent with me does not take into account the many hours I spent preparing your case.   I tried my best for you and made every effort to obtain the results you wanted.

Paul and his team took my evidence. They researched additional information and police reports. Paul met with Prosecuting attorney in negotiation several times. He settled my case outside of court I recommend his grit and common sense to stand up for his client while representing legal rights and his passion for what's fair

I would rate him a zero if I could. Basically did absolutely nothing for 30k....nothing! Zero communication and utter disorganization from this guy and his secretary. Thought he was a criminal defense attorney however after hiring him its as though he's still working for the DAs office. Don't be fooled. Definitely would not recommend him. "2023 update" In response to Paul Hermann's comments regarding my review one year later...not sure where to start. For Paul Hermann to go on social media and post that about my son "caused the death of a father  and a daughter and one small child to be a quadriplegic " is quite troubling in itself. Is this Paul Hermann the defense attorney or Paul Hermann the DA?  Because I was under the impression I was hiring a defense attorney....do the math folks. Pretty obvious which one I hired . On our initial consultation with Paul Hermann we were very fourth coming and point blank about what we were looking to achieve that we were unhappy with prior attorneys handling of our case and were not happy with the plea that prior attorney brought and presented to us. We sought Paul Hermann out because we felt there was not enough information and to many unanswered questions left from prior attorney and the plea that was offered was too harsh due to what we knew. Paul Hermann led us to believe that he could definitely get something done that there was way to many unanswered questions that a case of this nature should have a file 2' tall not 2" tall. I hired Paul Hermann on the premise that he was going to change or do his best due diligence to represent my son and minimize the sentence....he did absolutely no due diligence at all. My son wasn't facing Life Mr. Hermann! do your due diligence....the plea you brought him was the same as the first attorney ...you did nothing, however your post makes it look like you saved him from a life in Prison....you did nothing but offer him his prior plea from the previous attorney...my only regret was you duped me into giving 30,000 dollars and If I knew you felt he was guilty we could have walked away and and retained the legal services of a real defense attorney that would have done his best due diligence to represent my son. Do you really think I would have given you 30K if you had told me you thought my son was guilty?! Current attorney concurs there was no due diligence done on his case and outcome probably would have been the same with a public defender. Shame on you. Yes, I love my son and everyone deserves proper representation you sir did nothing.
reviews.ownerResponse: Kevin is the father of the adult client who loves his son very much.  The client caused the death of a father and a daughter and one small child to be a quadriplegic and the permanent injury of two other children.  Sometimes, that reality is hard to accept.  We worked very hard on the case and the plea was the right decision considering the adult client was facing prison for life.

And I was told if I wanted child support from the day he was ordered to pay child support on July 5th that I had to pay 3500$ to file an enforcement. reviews.ownerResponse: I am sorry that you are unhappy, Nancy!  I won your case for you and got you significant child support.  Your ex and his attorney have been very uncooperative, as you know.  I have answered all of your emails, including up to twelve emails in one day! You complain because your ex does not exercise his visitation, instead choosing to work overtime.  I explained that would ultimately result in you getting even more child support and that it also means you have extra time with your child.  You wanted to argue about it, even after I explained that you cannot file to hold someone in contempt for not exercising their visitation.  I have tried hard to cooperate with you and to be open and transparent about your case. I care about you and your child and worked hard on your behalf.  Even though you won your case, you are still unhappy with the outcome.  It is my goal in every case to help my clients achieve their goals, but even after I got you custody and a lot of child support, you have still been unhappy.   I'm sorry that I was not able to figure out what else you wanted me to accomplish! Best of luck to you in the future!

reviews.text: I just called, Im ready to pay for a lawyer but had a few questions I wanted answered first so that I'm able to choose the right one for my case.  I was told I need to pay 150 upfront to ask anything! I'm calling a couple of lawyers first before I make my decision. I want to know ratings ect. I asked the lady who answered and she almost seemed like she didn't know what I meant about her reviews with child support court.  All she kept getting at was the $150. If only a few answers would have been given to me so I could feel confident and trusting in the person I am thinking about hiring. I would of gladly paid but it seemed the lady who answered the phone wasn't aware and didn't know or want to answer me at all.  I understand you can't just get stuff for free but 3 questions maybe 4 just to give me an idea of what type of person she is shouldn't have to be charged. For this type of case we are putting not me but my children in someone else's hands and if they can't answer one question without $150 then it's about just money not taking the time to know and understand someones life and defend it.  To me that makes it to where as long as your paid win or lose it doesn't matter.  I'm not willing to take that risk.
reviews.ownerResponse: Rheanna, I am sorry that you were not satisfied with the responses you received.  We would love to have been able to help you!  We get calls all day long from people who just want free advice and do not actually want to hire an attorney.  I'm sorry that your questions were not answered, but the paralegal and the receptionist cannot, by law, give out legal advice.  I hope that your legal needs are being met and that you and your children are well and happy.  Best wishes to you!

Don’t trust him even if you payed him. I he believes these are libelous lies, I encourage him to take me to civil court and prove it because he can not.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Often when a person is charged with 3 cases of Burglary of a Habitation, a case of theft of a firearm, one case of Arson, and one case of selling drugs, the outcome isn’t what they hoped.
I’ll also point out that I got one of your burglary charges dropped, and for some reason you complained to the state about that. The fact that you complained that I got one of your cases dismissed says something.

When an individual’s criminal history in Randall County alone includes:
Arson (twice) retaliation, burglary of a habitation (twice) DWI, deadly conduct, theft of a firearm, and selling drugs, it can be difficult for an attorney to get the result the person wants, which is typically dismissal.

reviews.text: Mr. Enos did a great job on my divorce. I talked to other attorneys but decided to hire David because he spent a lot of time talking to me and really listened; instead of just passing me off to a paralegal.  Before I hired him my wife falsely accused me of domestic violence and had me thrown out of the house.  After I hired David she called CPS saying that I was abusive to our kids.  David worked with me through that ordeal and got the CPS case closed. In fact, David called the CPS investigator as a witness for me at trial and she testified that my wife was a bad mother and that I was a great Dad. reviews.text:
Would not recommend David Enos to anyone. Got everything situated before court and he ended up sitting with my ex wife before court started. Divorce decree stated for me to pay spousal support to my ex wife, and I did so per divorce decree. One year later they took my income tax money and they were saying I was behind. I had all my documents showing that I was all paid up and current. Mr. Enos told me he would go to the attorney general himself and handle it within 3 days. 3 weeks later he told me someone that was working there no longer worked there so he wasn't going to represent me anymore (state bar here I come). I would only recommend if you want to pay your ex spouse tons of money, sit with them before court, and then drop you to let you figure everything out on your own.

Update: This is not a fake review. Look for the name Esteban Ramirez. More than one person saw the whole sitting with my ex wife situation. As for the attorney general I had to do all the work on my own with an affidavit. Keep saying that this is fake but we both know the truth here. Call me if you have problems with this review, I'm sure you still have my number.
reviews.ownerResponse: Fake name - Fake review, and most likely posted by a competitor. None of our past clients have any unresolved problems with the Attorney General and we have never sat with a Client’s soon to be ex at a hearing. The Bible is full of stories where guys that went out on the limb to be God’s man were attacked by evil. That’s what is happening here.

reviews.text: David seems to be in the business because he genuinely cares.  I priced numerous lawyers for our step parent adoption with an unknown father. He was very devoted to our case, and everything went smoothly. I highly recommend his services to anyone!
reviews.ownerResponse: Adoptions are a real joy to do.  I really enjoyed being part of this great time in your family's live.

reviews.text: Worst lawyer I ever had told me I had a good case went to court right before we was about to go in and he said you might want to just settle
reviews.ownerResponse: Sorry for your situation.  If we advise a client to settle the day of a hearing, it is only after lengthy discussions with the other side and our client.  Local Judges require attorneys to visit before a hearing and try to resolve the matter without court intervention.  Sometimes a negotiated settlement between the parties is better than risking a ruling from the Judge that could be bad for everyone involved.  Family law cases are especially hard because settlement or trial, everyone usually leaves the courthouse angry and exhausted.

Since I picked up my court papers from this office location I was asked to review this office by Google. I also have almost 3 Million views on Google for reviews.

I'll just write about my own experience with her from the stand since she was not my attorney.

At first I was nervous. Then someone reminded me of something amazing, and it helped me all the way though my time on the stand. Prayer is powerful and God is good!!

I took a deep breathe, slowed down, and answered each thing she asked. If I didn't understand I would ask her to repeat it , or explain it. She was kind and did that for me.
At that point, Briar wasn't as intimidating as I expected. The hype of going against her was a bit of a joke honestly. I thought she would be this extremely business like professional. She came in looking like the girl on legally blonde with all the pink, and wearing clothes that seemed to be about 2 sizes too big. In his gallery, some  people couldn't help but laugh. I was entertained all day to say the least. She did not seem well organized as she kept skipping around on questions. My ADHD was like a bouncy ball trying to keep up with her questions. Some were even repetitive. At one point, I had a staring contest with someone from his gallery that was trying to intimidate me. I had to ask her to repeat the question. 😂
Again, she did.

The judge was patient and very kind with all involved. It was a very long day.

Now let's get to the deeper issue(s) I had with Briar.

In court,
Briar brought up the fact I advocate heavily, and with passion, for kids with special needs in schools as a problem. She brought up my speaking to the school board as "yelling". What she was referring to is on video, but she didnt play that part. She only mentioned the video to the judge to make me look bad. On the video, which she didn't mention this part to the judge, I had called out Robyn Cramner from CISD for laughing about a students suicide attempt.

I have two issues with this-

One, why would Briar not want someone to stand up for a minor child in crisis who couldn't do it themselves? A child who was on a 504,  multiple diagnostic issues, and needed someone to stand up for their advocacy. I find that odd that a friend of the courts wouldn't want that for kids.

Two, that video being used at any point of questioning should have been disclosed in the discovery phase,  but was not to my knowledge.

I will continue to use the training I received from Region 16, The Pen Project, and other places. I will continue advocacy for kids with special needs in all the public school districts of Texas as needed.

Meanwhile, I hope that one day schools and others learn to respect those who advocate for the least of these.

After all that's what the Bible commands us to do. 🩷🩷🩷

reviews.text:
I’ve waited several months before making this review pending the outcome of our case. I originally asked for Briar to take our case. However, at the time her case load was full and our consultation was done with Audrey Sirmon. My husband(respondent)  was going back to court for a child support modification. We were looking to pay an additional $1100 a month per the Texas child support guidelines. Anytime we asked a question during the consultation, Audrey was just simply state “There is already a stipulation for that in your current order.”

Yes we know what the current order says we are here for a MODIFICATION!! So very frustrating to be brushed off. Anyways, long story short, we ended up going with Abbott Law WHO IS ABSOLUTELY AMAZING and we won our case.

I hate to leave a low star review because I hear great things about Briar. But Audrey however was very disappointing.
reviews.ownerResponse: Abbott is great lawyer - glad it worked out for you.

reviews.text: Destroys my phone with spam calls and text constantly about an outstanding balance I owe I don’t owe tickets and anything I’ve had has been paid. Fake attorneys fake law firm fake scam texts and emails they literally have a 1 on google how much worse can your reputation get lol a year of the same text spamming my phone this should be harassment I finally pressed stop after reading the entire thing horrible

SCAM ARTISTS!!!! My husband received a nasty post card in the mail last Friday for a ticket he had already paid in Childress county and this “law firm” insisted he owed another $343.90 for failure to appear when in fact Childress county said everything was good on their end! SO MAKE SURE YOU CALL WHER EVER THEY ARE CLAIMING YOU OWE MOMEY TO BEFORE YOU PAY THESE THIEVES ANY MONEY!!!

I just called trying to figure out if I owed tax money and the secretary was rude to me and said" well if you don't own property then you don't have to pay". Well stop sending me letters saying I owe you money. I still really don't know what this place is for but hopefully you don't owe them money and if you do call the rude secretary an make sure. Apparently they don't know who owes them money. Be careful

Taking collections on something that was voted unconstitutional by Texas few years ago. (Red light cameras) Amarillo just only barely ended the contract in sept but still want their last penny by threatening it’s residents with huge illegal red light camera fraud via this trash bag collection agency.  They even go as far to threaten a civil suit against someone who doesn’t even reside in that town.  Who audits the audit? Who’s  going after these crooks? Contact your state rep immediately

They used my credit card information to pay someone else’s ticket to a court house in a different state and now they want me to pay additional fees for their mistake. I’m still out the money they used for the first transaction. Bunch of thieves!!!

They did not respect my request to stop contacting me, which is illegal under our laws. Instead of sending me 1 demand to pay a debt, they sent me 2. 2 duplicates of the same one, 4 letters total. I have submitted a complaint to Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) (the government agency that helps enforce this disregard for consumer laws) and I highly recommend anyone else being harrassed by this company do the same and seek damages if they do not stop.

These scumbags wanna use threatening letters to scare you to pay red light tickets which were ruled unconstitutional in the state of Texas back in 2019. Texas Transportation Code 707.021, “A local authority may not issue a civil or criminal charge or citation for an offense or violation based on a recorded image produced by a photographic traffic signal enforcement system.”

This place is a joke. A judgment for money for writing a bad check from 2009. I called them wanting more info since WHO can remember anything from 13 years ago. After getting the run around, it would cost me a sum of money to even get a copy of the check from this place, of course. Statute of limitations is well exceeded by Texas law. Announcements of all of this from a prepaid post card. SCAM!!!!! Now stop the harassment!!

I was sent a letter from this place, Called was put on hold for over 10 minutes, the rep said sorry told about the letter, to make matters worse, she said they don’t take payments, ask to speak to someone there who cares, she said there’s no there that care......Obviously
Why send a letter to make payment but don’t even take the payments.
according to the justice of the peace in Amarillo, this is indeed their collection agent. They are not a court.
This is a scam. They dont even have an office in the building.

These folks are well known scam artists. The P.O. Box they provided that is supposedly where I was told to send a money order is not even near the correct address for my local municipal court. That and a municipal court doesn't even use a P.O. Box. Nice try, jerks.

My name is Stacy Findley.  I received a call from your office this morning informing me that I owe you money for a very old citation out of Potter County.  When I told your collections girl that this had been paid last May (2017), because I wasn't able to renew my driver's license, obtain insurance, etc., until it was paid and I have renewed and obtained the aforementioned things.
She rudely told me that I did, in fact, still owe the money and that it "will definitely show back up on my license...".  I called the court and was told that this matter was, in fact, indeed, taken care of last May (2017).
You people need to get your business straight and STOP hounding people who have paid their debt(s)!!!

Wish I could give more than 5 stars. I’d been stuck on Probation with a pending Motion to Revoke for 5 years (alleged marijuana possession). I hired  Adam Tisdell of the Tisdell Law Firm as local counsel; Adam took over the case and went to work digging into every facet of the law that potentially applied and was able to get the DA to dismiss the Motion to Revoke by playing hardball and demanding accountability from the state. While other lawyers like to take the easy route and plea out rather than put in the work to hold the state accountable to their burden of proof at trial, Adam and the Tisdell Law Firm will always fight to get the best outcome for you. When your life is on the line, don’t make the mistake of picking a lawyer that isn’t willing to put in the work to fight for you.

But then I found the Tisdell Law Firm and Adam Tisdell agreeded to take my case out of the kindness of his heart. Adam Tisdell is a true advocate for his clients. It was a complete disaster and this man took the time to listen to my testimony and upon discovery was able to have all the charges dismissed. This man is not your average attorney. reviews.text: First and foremost I would like to thank Adam Tisdell's parents for raising an incredible angel. I was pulled over and taken to jail in Oldham County, Texas  for possession of controlled substance (marijuana). My car was profiled with Arizona plates at the time. I went to jail and bail was 30,000 bond-able. I was able to bail out but I was nervous and thought I would serve some real prison time. I called Adam after seeing his billboard several times, after being released from jail. Once we spoke he reassured me that I would be okay, I felt more relaxed. On my way driving to Texas I had fallen asleep and ruined two of my tires, thankfully that was all I ruined. Adam did more than I could ask for on that day, he literally brought tears to my eyes. reviews.text: Highly recommend Eric Pullen. He was great at answering all of our questions. He was very patient and understanding, and he made the process very easy. Would recommend him to anyone.
Eric and his office are very professional and courteous. Thank you for all your help !  You’re the best!
We were very happy with the work that he did for us.
Eric is very knowledgeable and professional.

GREAT experience with Eric. Working in Senior Living I have seen wills and things not go as planned so often and understand the need for a professional specialty attorney to draft your documents. Eric was thorough, kind, and just generally caring about our situation and process. Definately reccomend him!

A must read review…
Mr. Pullen drew up my Mom’s Last Will & Testament, Durable POA, Medical POA, etc… in 2011 just prior to her cancer diagnosis. She had many questions about the process, but he was patient and thoroughly explained everything for her. After her passing, I made an appointment to make sense of the process I needed to handle the estate; he was nothing shy of professional, compassionate, and remarkably easy to work with.  He took care of everything! I simply appeared before the judge while he did the rest.

As many people realize, handling an estate after someone’s death can truly bring out the worst in family members when not everyone agrees with final wishes. Part of this, I believe, is sometimes more simple than greed because we all handle grief in our own unique ways! This was something that my mother anticipated, and the driving force behind making arrangements in advance of becoming ill. There was, indeed, a no contest clause; however, there are still ways for others to delay the probate and closing of an estate.

My brothers decided to contest various aspects of the process regarding the role my mother asked me to take. It wasn’t anything based in fact, but it caused delays, including the possibility of a trial, but eventually led to a mediated resolution. Eric was knowledgeable, empathetic, and was on the ball keeping everything progressing for a resolution as quickly as possible!

You rely on having everything properly set in place while taking the time to truly listen to your unique circumstances. I know how much comfort that brought to my mom; I will be forever grateful for how he interacted with my mom. I just had no idea that I would end up seeking out his advice and services as well after she passed away. Anyone can draw up documents for you final wishes, including yourself, if you’re bold / confident enough to think that you will remember and anticipate all possibilities that will unfold during an exceptionally difficult period of your life. Being the Executrix, it can be a bit stressful and unnerving simply because it isn’t something you do everyday. Eric guided and reassured us from start to finish! The professional and compassionate man he and his staff are, completely makes all the difference in the world!

I sincerely appreciate everything he did for my mom in getting her affairs in order! After her passing, I opted to seek out his counsel for the probate process as he was familiar with the details of the estate and what wishes my mom personally expressed to him. I cannot tell you how valuable his knowledge and expertise was to my mother and I! Mr. Pullen, it’s been 14 years since you guided us through this journey, and I will always remember and appreciate you! I’ve since referred everyone I know who is looking to get their affairs in order, and everyone I hear that is going to be in charge of matters for a loved one during the final days of care, as well as with the process of probate and closing of an estate!

You have many options for who you choose to draw up documents for yourself or your family, but the person you choose to guide you, and walk through the process for beginning to end is an invaluable resource! I cannot recommend Eric Pullen strongly enough!! Sir, you will forever be remembered for walking beside me during one of the most difficult times of my life!
Good legal advice and easy to work with.

10/10 would recommend.  Eric is very knowledgeable about all aspects of the law and contracts.  He's courteous and timely with any needs that come up.  If you're in need of estate planning law, wills, or trusts, you can't go wrong with this firm.
